title: Blood on Upholstery
stainChemistry: Blood on upholstery is the same protein chemistry as on fabric — blood proteins (blood pigment and others) — but upholstery has three construction concerns that fabric doesn't: (1) you can't remove the fabric from the frame, so you can't flush from the back; (2) stuffing (foam, down, polyester batting) sits directly under the fabric and holds water indefinitely; (3) the visible surface is often a blend (cotton-poly, rayon-poly, microfiber) with unknown dye-fastness. Cloth-transfer + aggressive extraction is non-negotiable; saturation causes fabric-to-frame water marks and permanent stuffing odor.
whyThisWorks: Cold water + cloth-transfer handles fresh blood without reaching the stuffing. Liquid Protein Spotter on a cloth then lifts any residue the water couldn't — never sprinkle powder on upholstery; dry powder works into the weave and is nearly impossible to fully remove. 3% hydrogen peroxide on a cloth for stubborn shadow, but always test hidden (under-cushion / inside-seam) first — upholstery dyes are often not colorfast.
defaultAssumption: Assume unknown fiber blend and unknown colorfastness. Test every applied product on an under-cushion or inside-seam area first.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Care tag on the cushion seam
spottingProtocol.instruction: Find the furniture cleaning code on the tag (usually under a cushion): W (water-safe), S (solvent-only, no water), WS (either), X (vacuum only — professional required). If the code is S or X, stop here and route to a professional upholstery cleaner — no home water treatment is safe. If W or WS, test any product you're about to apply on a hidden area first (back, under-cushion seam).

spottingProtocol.equipment: Clean white terry towels or paper towels
spottingProtocol.instruction: Fresh blood: press a folded white towel straight down on the stain. Lift. Move to a dry section. Press again. Don't rub — rubbing works blood sideways into the weave. Dried blood: gently scrape off surface crust with the edge of a spoon or dull butter knife before any liquid touches it.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Spray bottle, clean white cloth, wet/dry vac or dry towels
spottingProtocol.instruction: Lightly dampen a clean white cloth with cold water — do not saturate. Press onto the stain, lift, rotate to a clean section of cloth, press again. Blood transfers onto the cloth. Between cycles, press a DRY towel down with weight for 30 seconds to pull moisture back out before it reaches the stuffing. Stop saturating the moment the cloth comes away almost clean — don't keep going until the fabric looks soaked.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Liquid Protein Spotter or dissolved enzyme pretreat
spottingProtocol.instruction: For any shadow that remains: apply liquid Protein Spotter (PRENETT B or a dissolved enzyme pretreat) to a clean white cloth — not to the upholstery. Press onto the stain. Let it dwell 5–10 minutes. Extract hard with a dry weighted towel. If you only have powder enzyme pretreat, dissolve it fully in cold water first — never sprinkle powder on upholstery.

spottingProtocol.technique: Drop on cotton swab, test, then spot
spottingProtocol.instruction: For residual pink or brown shadow: test a drop of 3% hydrogen peroxide on a hidden area (back of a cushion, inside seam) first — upholstery dyes vary wildly and peroxide can lighten the fabric itself. If the test passes: dab peroxide on a cotton swab, touch the stain, watch for lift. Flush cool with a damp cloth as soon as the shadow clears.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Neutral Synthetic Detergent on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Damp cloth with NSD, press onto the treated area to pull out residual enzyme and peroxide. Extract hard with a dry weighted towel. Residual chemistry trapped in upholstery + stuffing = odor, yellowing, and long-term fabric damage.

spottingProtocol.technique: Slow dry, no heat, prevent water-ring
spottingProtocol.instruction: Place a folded dry towel on the spot with a weight for 30 minutes to keep pulling moisture up. Point a fan at the area. Air-dry thoroughly — no hair-dryer, no direct sun (fades dye), no sitting on it. Upholstery dries slowly because stuffing holds moisture; rushing with heat causes fabric-to-frame water rings and musty odor.

steps: 1. Check the cleaning code (W/S/WS/X). S or X = specialist. Test hidden area.
2. Blot fresh / scrape dry — lift bulk before any liquid.
3. Cold water on damp cloth, press-and-lift, extract with dry weighted towel between cycles.
4. Liquid Protein Spotter on cloth for residue. Dwell 5–10 min. Extract.
5. 3% H₂O₂ on swab for shadow — test hidden area first, flush cool via cloth.
6. NSD rinse via cloth, aggressive extraction.
7. Fan-dry with weighted towel. No heat, no sun, several hours.
products: Liquid Protein Spotter (PRENETT B or equivalent)
Heavy absorbent white towels, wet/dry vac if available
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — hot water sets protein and can damage dye + backing
warnings: Never pour liquid on upholstery — stuffing holds moisture indefinitely
Never skip the hidden-area test — upholstery dyes often not colorfast
Never use heat to speed drying — water rings, musty odor, dye fade
Never sprinkle enzyme powder on upholstery — cannot fully remove
Cleaning-code X or S = professional only

homeSolutions.instruction: Most upholstered furniture has a cleaning code on a tag under the cushion. W means water-safe. S means solvent-only (no water). WS means either. X means vacuum only, call a pro. If it's S or X, stop here and call an upholstery cleaner — home water methods will damage the fabric.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Don't guess the cleaning code — code X + water = permanent fabric damage.

homeSolutions.agent: Test a Hidden Area Before You Touch the Stain
homeSolutions.instruction: Before you apply anything to the visible stain, test on a hidden spot — back of a cushion, inside a seam. Dampen a cloth with cold water, press it on the hidden area for 30 seconds. Check for color transfer or a water ring after it dries. If either happens, stop and call a pro.

Fresh blood: press a folded white towel straight down on the stain. Lift, move to a dry section, press again. No rubbing — rubbing works blood sideways into the weave and into the stuffing underneath. Dried blood: scrape the crust off with the edge of a spoon before any liquid.

Lightly dampen a clean white cloth with cold water. The cloth should feel cool and slightly damp, not wet. Press onto the stain, lift, rotate to a clean section, repeat. Blood transfers onto the cloth. Never pour water on the upholstery — the stuffing underneath will hold it for hours and cause water rings and musty smell.

After every 3–4 cloth presses, switch to a thick DRY towel. Press down hard — put a book or heavy pan on top for 30 seconds. The dry towel pulls moisture out before it reaches the stuffing. Check the dry towel for blood — you'll often see it transferring. Swap to a fresh section, repeat.

For stubborn shadow: dissolve an enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, or Zout) in cold water per the label. Apply to a cloth, press onto the stain, let sit 10 minutes, extract with a dry weighted towel. Never sprinkle powder directly on upholstery — it gets into the weave and is almost impossible to fully remove.

homeSolutions.agent: Hydrogen Peroxide 3% on a Swab — After Hidden Test
homeSolutions.instruction: Test a drop of 3% hydrogen peroxide on that same hidden area first. If the fabric doesn't lighten, dab peroxide on a cotton swab and touch the stain. Watch closely — peroxide can lift blood in under a minute. Flush with a cool damp cloth the moment the shadow clears.

homeSolutions.agent: Fan Dry, Don't Sit on It
homeSolutions.instruction: Point a fan at the spot. Put a folded dry towel on it with something heavy for 30 minutes. Let the cushion air-dry fully before sitting on it — several hours to overnight. Sitting on damp upholstery presses moisture into the stuffing and can create a permanent water ring on the outside fabric. No hair dryers, no heat — dries unevenly and fades dye.

Once fully dry, check: any musty smell, water ring around the cleaned spot, or visible stain remaining? Call an upholstery cleaner. Tell them: "Blood on upholstery, cleaning code [W/WS], age [time], home-treated with cold water + enzyme pretreat + 3% hydrogen peroxide on a cloth, no pouring, no heat."

steps: 1. Find the furniture cleaning code. S / X = professional only.
2. Test cold water + any product on a hidden area (back of cushion) first.
3. Blot fresh / scrape dry bulk — no rubbing.
4. Cold water on a damp cloth, press-and-lift. Never pour.
5. Dry weighted towel between wet passes to pull moisture up.
6. Shadow: enzyme pretreat dissolved in cold water, applied via cloth, 10-min dwell.
7. Stubborn shadow: 3% hydrogen peroxide on a swab after hidden test.
8. Fan-dry fully before sitting on it. No heat.
products: Enzyme laundry pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, or Zout) dissolved in cold water
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Cleaning-code S or X. Water ring after drying. Musty odor. Two home rounds without clearing. Call an upholstery cleaner.
warnings: Never pour liquid on upholstery — stuffing holds moisture and causes rings
Never skip the hidden-area colorfast test — upholstery dyes often lift
Never sprinkle enzyme powder on upholstery
No heat drying — rings, fade, musty odor

neverDo: Never pour liquid on upholstery — stuffing failure mode
Never skip hidden-area colorfast test — most upholstery dye is not fully fast
Never apply heat to damp upholstery — water rings + musty odor
Never sprinkle enzyme powder directly — cannot fully remove
Cleaning-code S or X: home work not safe
fiberSensitivities: Upholstery blends vary — test every product on a hidden area first
Stuffing holds moisture far longer than the visible fabric — extraction is non-negotiable
Dye colorfastness is not a given on upholstery — peroxide and enzyme can lighten fabric

materialWarnings: Check the W/S/WS/X cleaning code on the tag first
Test every product on a hidden area (back of cushion) before the stain
Transfer liquid via cloth only — never pour
No heat drying — musty odor and water rings

escalation.when: Cleaning-code S or X, water ring after drying, musty odor, blood older than 24–48 hours on porous upholstery, or two home rounds without clearing.
escalation.whatToTell: "Blood on upholstery, cleaning code [W/WS/S/X], age [time], home-treated with cold water + enzyme pretreat + 3% hydrogen peroxide on a cloth, no pouring, no heat."

contextQuestions: What's the furniture cleaning code (W, S, WS, X)?
What's the fabric — cotton, polyester blend, microfiber, velvet, leather?
Any hot water or heat already applied?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Cleaning code X or S = specialist every time. Velvet and silk-blend upholstery escalates early. Synthetic microfiber + W code + fresh blood is the easiest DIY case.
